ocr.text: The integration of digital wallets within the embedded finance landscape is rapidly evolving, particularly in the European
market. As illustrated in our data, the adoption curve reflects a significant shift towards digital payment methods, with notable
metrics such as a projected market penetration increase of 20% by Q22023. This trend is further evidenced by the upward
trajectory in units sold, which is set to rise from 500K to 700K, demonstrating growing consumer acceptance.
AdoptionRate  UserGrowth  MonthlyTransactions  AverageAmount
[HIDDEN]  30%  1.5M  [HIDDEN]  £55
TotalUsers  5M  ActiveUsers  3M  InactiveUsers
2M  GrowthRate  25%  Avg.Transaction  [HIDDEN]
Additionally, customer satisfaction levels remain notably high at 90%, which plays a crucial role in driving customer retention,
currently at 75%.
EmbeddedFinanceMetrics  TransactionVolume  AverageTicketSize  CustomerEngagement
NewAccounts  ActiveUsers  ConversionRate  NetRevenue  GrossMargin  OperatingProfit
UnitsSold  Headcount  NumberofBranches  CustomerRetention  FraudRate  ChargebackRate  CustomerSatisfaction  MarketPenetration  GeographicReach
20%  [HIDDEN]  [HIDDEN]  85%  0.1%  0.3%  [HIDDEN]  30%  15
The financial implications are also substantial; for instance, average transaction values are expected to increase, with the
average value per transaction rising from £50 in Q12023 to £57 by Q22023. Such metrics underscore the importance of robust
strategies in managing these shifts, especially considering the projected increase in headcount to 1,500 to support this growth.
Furthermore, as companies expand their geographic reach, maintaining a low fraud rate will be essential, particularly in light of
the expected chargeback rate fluctuations. Overall, the data highlights the need for adaptive strategies that align with the
evolving landscape of digital payments and consumer preferences.
